DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT NETWORK GENERAL CENTRAL CORPORATION, MICHAEL POPE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS THE RESPONSES OBJECT TO ANY REQUEST AS THE REQUEST SEEKS DOCUMENTS THAT FALL WITHIN THE WORK PRODUCT AND ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE, THOSE DOCUMENTS MUST BE IDENTIFIED WITH PARTICULARITY IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. PLAINTIFF MUST RESPOND FURTHER WITH THAT PRIVILEGE LOG WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF NO DOCUMENTS ARE BEING WITHHELD PURSUANT TO A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE, THE OBJECTIONS MUST BE WITHDRAWN. AS THE MOTION SEEKS TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AFTER THE MERGER OCCURRED, THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT IS IRRELEVANT. HOWEVER, AS DOCUMENTS THAT WERE CREATED POST-MERGER MAY REFER TO PRE-MERGER OPINIONS AND ASSUMPTIONS, THOSE DOCUMENTS ARE RELEVANT AND MUST BE PRODUCED. THE MOTION IS ALSO DENIED AS TO NUMBER 46 AS IT SEEKS TO INVADE THE TAX RETURN PRIVILEGE AND AS IT IS IRRELEVANT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS NETWORK GENERAL CENTRAL CORPORATION, AND MICHAEL POPE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS NETWORK GENERAL CENTRAL CORPORATION AND MICHAEL POPE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE PAROLE EVIDENCE RULE IS NOT A BAR TO THESE CLAIMS. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE FRAUD IN THE INDUCEMENT AND THE $3 PER SHARE ALLOCATIONS DO NOT CONTRADICT THE AGREEMENT, BUT MERELY ADDS A VALUE TO THE STOCK. ECONOMIC LOSS RULE DOES NOT APPLY BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT INCLUDES CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON TORT, NOT CONTRACT. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WHETHER ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATIONS ARE EXPRESSIONS OF OPINION OR AFFIRMATIONS OF FACT. POPE HAD SUPERIOR KNOWLEDGE REGARDING THE SUBJECT MATTER AND REPRESENTED THE $3 PRICE AS A FACT. AS TO THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION THAT THE STRIKE PRICE WOULD BE CHARGED TO $3, TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT RAISED WHETHER POPE BELIEVED THE PRICE WOULD BE INCREASED CONSIDERING THE OTHER INFORMATION POPE POSSESSED, SUCH AS THE INTERNAL EVALUATION CONDUCTED BY DUFF AND PHELPS. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING PLAINTIFFS' RELIANCE. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS WERE SOPHISTICATED INVESTORS, POPE GAVE A REASONABLE EXPLANATION AS TO WHY THE $3 PRICE COULD NOT BE INCLUDED IN THE AGREEMENT (TAX IMPLICATIONS). THE COURT ADDS: SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED AS TO PLAINTIFFS ADAMS COHEN, FREEDOM HILL CAPITAL AND MR. CUNNINGHAM AS WELL. ALL EVID. OBJECT'S OVERRULED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Charles Kagay, Atty. for Plaintiff and opposing party: JT Ventures Partners, Cochin Fund, LP

AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS NETWORK GENERAL CENTRAL CORPORATION, MICHAEL POPE, KENNETH HAO, GREG MONDRE, BRYAN TAYLOR, JOHN MARREN, WILLIAM GIBSON, ROBERT SCHLOSSMAN'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents Pursuant To Court Order And Request For Sanctions. The court grants defendants' motion and orders plaintiffs further to respond to defendants; request for production of documents, as set forth in the form of order submitted by defendants, The court imposes a monetary sanction against plaintiffs JT Venture Partners Alleppey Fund L.P., J.T. Venture Partners Cochin Fund, L.P., Windspeed Ventures, Freedom Hill Capital LC., and James Cunningham, and their counsel, of $4,090, payable to defendants. Plaintiffs to comply within 20 days as to each request. Plaintiffs compliance may take the form of production of more documents or assurance in the form of a declaration under penalty of perjury that plaintiffs have produced all the documents called for which exist. JUDGE WALLACE P. DOUGLASS NOT REPORTED.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANTS NETWORK GENERAL CENTRAL CORPORATION AND MICHAEL POPE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Defendants Network General Central Corporation And Michael Pope'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Transferred From Department 301)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Defendants Network General Central Corporation and Michael Pope's Motion For Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication. Appearances: Robert B. Lovett, Esq. & Mazda K. Anita, Esq. of Cooley Godward Kronish LLP (858) 550-6139 appearing for Defendants and Moving party; Charles M. Kagay, Esq. of Spiegel Liao & Kagay LLP (415) 956-5959 appearing for Plaintiffs; Gerald Jay Resnick, Esq. of Resnick Nirenberg & Cash, P.C. (973) 781-1204 appearing for Plaintiffs. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication is denied. The Parole evidence rule is not a bar to these claims. Plaintiffs allege fraud in the inducement and the $3 per share allocations do not contradict the agreement, but merely adds a value to the stock. Economic loss rule does not apply because plaintiff's complaint includes causes of action based on tort, not contract. Triable issue of fact whether alleged misrepresentations are expressions of opinion or affirmations of fact. Pope had superior knowledge regarding the subject matter and represented the $3 price as a fact. As to the alleged misrepresentation that the strike price would be charged to $3, triable issue of fact raised whether Pope believed the price would be increased considering the other information Pope possessed, such as the internal evaluation conducted by Duff and Phelps. Triable issue of fact regarding plaintiffs' reliance. Although plaintiffs were sophisticated investors, Pope gave a reasonable explanation as to why the $3 price could not be included in the agreement (tax implications). The Court adds: Summary Judgment/Summary adjudication denied as to Plaintiffs Adams Cohen, Freedom Hill Capital and Mr. Cunningham as well. All evid. object's overruled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Amended Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Oct-05-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
MAZDA K. ANTIA, ESQ., (858)550-6139, Defendants/Moving Parties, Network General Central Corporation, Michael Pope
CHARLES M. KAGAY, ESQ., (415)956-5959, FOR PLAINTIFFS/OPPOSING PARTIES

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant Network General Central Corporation, Michael Pope's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. As the responses object to any request as the request seeks documents that fall within the work product and attorney client privilege, those documents must be identified with particularity in a privilege log. Plaintiff must respond further with that privilege log within 15 days of notice of this order. If no documents are being withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, the objections must be withdrawn. As the motion seeks to compel production of documents after the merger occurred, the motion is denied as it is irrelevant. However, as documents that were created post-merger may refer to pre-merger opinions and assumptions, those documents are relevant and must be produced. The motion is also denied as to Number 46 as it seeks to invade the tax return privilege and as it is irrelevant. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED. (EAH/612)
